title: Mirror-y equivariance for cylinder-flow velocity fields
transformation_preconditions: The computational domain and boundary labels are symmetric under y reflection.
Velocity components use a known x/y ordering.
The source case remains within the intended cylinder-flow regime after reflection.

boundary_condition_compatibility.reason: The transformation is physically meaningful only when geometry, boundary labels, and vector-component conventions are mirror-compatible.

output_mapping.description: Compare the mirrored follow-up velocity after mapping v -> -v.

exclusion_rules: Exclude cases with asymmetric boundary labels.
Exclude cases with unknown vector-component ordering.
Classify transformed cases outside declared preconditions as out-of-relation-domain.

verdict_interpretation.pass: The observed run satisfied mirror-y equivariance under the declared tolerance.
verdict_interpretation.fail: The observed run exceeded tolerance under stated mirror-compatible conditions.
verdict_interpretation.skip: Prerequisites or runtime artifacts were unavailable.
verdict_interpretation.out-of-relation-domain: Mirror compatibility preconditions were not met.
verdict_interpretation.numerical-tolerance-issue: The result was dominated by numeric or determinism uncertainty.
verdict_interpretation.inconclusive: The evidence was insufficient for pass/fail classification.

claim_limitations: This card is a design-time OOD-stress asset. It does not report SUT execution or assert that mirror-y is valid for all cylinder-flow encodings.
